verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain and is a soluble protein, but it is detected in sperm as part of larger complexes with TM IZUMO family members (notably IZUMO3) and is localized to the acrosomal region. Studies report IZUMO4-JUNO interactions and antibody blockade of fertilization, implying surface accessibility on acrosome-reacted/germline cells. Display is restricted to the sperm/germline lineage and tied to acrosomal exocytosis, so the protein body reaches the outer face only in a narrow developmental context.
